title: Co-Design, Equity, and Accessible Participation · Decision Retrieval
summary: A delayed review reconstructs co-design, equity, and accessible participation from stakeholder, decision, assumption, revision, artifact, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, owner, and residual-risk evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for co-design plan and equity impact record after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose inform, consult, involve, collaborate, or shared-decision participation deliberately and communicate its actual influence.
Budget format, assistive technology, language, schedule, compensation, connectivity, cognitive load, and support accommodations before recruitment.
Compare benefits, burdens, error rates, exclusion, recourse, and resource demands across relevant groups without inventing demographic certainty.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Participation Level · changed case · Review Participation Level
steps.instruction:
review case — Affected people are invited after scope is fixed, receive inaccessible materials, and never learn which input changed the decision. The review variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Choose inform, consult, involve, collaborate, or shared-decision participation deliberately and communicate its actual influence.

Which action demonstrates Participation Level without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Participation Level · changed case · Review Participation Level.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose inform, consult, involve, collaborate, or shared-decision participation deliberately and communicate its actual influence. For Participation Level · changed case · Review Participation Level, keep Participation Level connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

stimulus.title: Feedback Accountability case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Affected people are invited after scope is fixed, receive inaccessible materials, and never learn which input changed the decision. The review variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Report what input changed, did not change, why, who decided, and how participants can challenge or withdraw.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Collecting feedback completes the obligation to participants.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Report what input changed, did not change, why, who decided, and how participants can challenge or withdraw. Artifact under review: co-design plan and equity impact record.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Feedback Accountability, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Record what required effort · Review Feedback Accountability
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Feedback Accountability: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Affected people are invited after scope is fixed, receive inaccessible materials, and never learn which input changed the decision. The review variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Report what input changed, did not change, why, who decided, and how participants can challenge or withdraw.

Evidence target: Record what required effort · Review Feedback Accountability.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Record what required effort · Review Feedback Accountability, keep Feedback Accountability connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Report what input changed, did not change, why, who decided, and how participants can challenge or withdraw.
